MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Bruce Feder, court-appointed counsel for Rosia Lee Hart.
Teresa Green for successor trustee Margaret Pinckard (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Status on Transfer of Accounting Materials.

Ms. Greene reported that efforts had been made to obtain the accounting materials from former successor trustee Paula Bibbs, but that the draft accounting and supporting documentation had not yet been received. She stated that Ms. Bibbs had been hospitalized and undergone surgery, which had delayed the process.

The Court noted that, at the prior hearing, it had been reported that approximately 80 percent of the accounting had been completed, but that those materials had not been transmitted. The Court continued the status hearing to February 11, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The Court stated it would issue an Order to Appear to attorney Dennis Vann to appear at the continued hearing, as well as an Order to Show Cause re sanctions in the amount of $250 for failure to appear and failure to transmit the accounting materials.

Off Calendar Dec-15-2025 Continued To Mar-16-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Dennis Vann for former successor trustee, Paula Bibbs (via Zoom).
Bruce Feder, court-appointed counsel for Rosia Hart (via Zoom).
Robert Little for trustee, Margaret Pinckard (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Status on Filing 4th Account.

Mr. Vann reported that Ms. Bibbs had completed approximately 80% of the accounting but subsequently suffered a stroke and was unable to complete the remaining work. Mr. Little stated that the current trustee was willing to step in and complete the accounting if necessary. Mr. Feder suggested it would be more cost-effective for the current trustee to complete the accounting rather than awaiting further progress from Ms. Bibbs.

The Court agreed that it would be in the best interests of all parties, including Ms. Bibbs, for the accounting materials to be transferred so the accounting could be completed without further delay. The Court ordered Ms. Bibbs, through counsel, to transmit the draft accounting and all supporting documentation to Ms. Pinckard, through counsel.

The Court set a status hearing on transmission of the draft accounting and backup materials for January 14, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The Court continued the status hearing regarding filing of the fourth account to March 16, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Dennis Vann, for Paula Bibbs, Trustee.
Bruce Feder, court-appointed counsel for Rosia Hart, Trustor.

Petition For Order Approving Second Amendment To Rosia L Hart Revocable Trust Dated May 19 2004 And Pour Over Will And Request To Remove Trust From Court Supervision
Off Calendar Jan-15-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Schultz for prior Trustee.
Bruce Feder for and with Rosia Hart.
Paula Bibbs in Propria Persona.

Ms. Schultz stated that he has spoken with Mr. Thomas and Mr. Conrad, that the only interest they all have has to do with outstanding fees, that they do not take any position regarding the proposed change in Beneficiaries and that they only object to the change in the Trustee provision. Mr. Schultz also stated that Ms. Hart was a victim of elder abuse, that the conservatorship was necessary because the abuser continued to have access to her for many years, that the trust is very much a substitute for a conservatorship, that he prefers that a Professional remain in place and that the trust remain under Court supervision. Mr. Feder stated that Ms. Hart is doing very well, that Ms. Hart is 100 years old and still lives in her home, that she cleans and cooks for herself, that she has formed a good relationship with Ms. Bibbs, and that she understands what has happened to her and has her guard up. Mr. Feder also stated that the trust is working well, that Ms. Bibbs is not intending on going anywhere, that Ms. Hart has had a very good support system around her and that he sees no reason to continue the expense of Court supervision. Ms. Bibbs stated that she and Ms. Hart have a great relationship, that Ms. Hart is very active and has Friends, and that if Ms. Hart has any suspicion of anyone, she knows to call her. Mr. Feder stated that he believes Mr. Schultz, Mr. Thomas and Mr. Conrad's interests are well protected and that they have liens against the property. Ms. Hart stated that she wants to be free to dance and do her thing. The Court questions how much the costs are to keep the trust under Court supervision. Mr. Feder estimates at least $5,000.00 for the accountings and maybe more depending on what needs to be done, stated that his Client doesn't want Court supervision, that he does not feel there is a need for it and raised the issue of whether the Court would consider only having supervision with regard to any changes in the Trustees and it having to be approved by the Court. Mr. Schultz stated that he would be more comfortable with keeping the trust under Court supervision. The Petition for Order Approving Second Amendment to Rosia L. Hart Revocable Trust, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Gomez. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-16-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-06-288756 Conservatorship of Rosie Lee Hart.

Appearances: Bruce Feder for and with Rosia Lee Hart (Conservatee). Lawrence Siracusa for and with Herb Thomas (Petitioner/Trustee/Conservator).

Mr. Feder stated that in general his Client objects to the Conservatorship, that she strongly believes that she can manage her own financial affairs and that she objects to the costs of the Conservatorship. With respect to the Petition for Instructions, the Court asked what the Trustees recommendations are. Mr. Siracusa explained that they did their request in a petition for instructions because his Client has tried really hard to work with Ms. Hart, but that there has been difficulty. The Parties were asked to step out of the courtroom and discuss the matter. Mr. Feder advised the Court that his Client asked him to petition to terminate the Conservatorship and that he was prepared to do so. When the Parties returned, Mr. Siracusa stated that they should keep the Antioch property even though it was underwater because the Conservatee resides there, that his Client believes that the Newhall property should be sold because there are significant problems with the property, that the vacant portion of the property attracts vandals/graffiti, and suggested that the other portion of the Petition for Instructions be continued to the hearing date on the Petition to Terminate. In addition, Mr. Siracusa stated that if the Petition to Terminate is not granted that Ms. Hart has someone else in mind, that his Client is not opposed to stepping down as long as it is a licensed Professional Fiduciary, that Mr. Thomas would like the authority to not pay the mortgage on the Suisun property and allow it to go into foreclosure and that the Hercules property should be deeded back to Mr. Wiggins as it is deeply under water. Mr. Feder stated that his Client strongly opposes sale of the Newhall property, that she would like to have the Petition for Instructions considered after the Petition to Terminate is decided, and that he is not sure what Ms. Hart would do with the properties but that she would like the opportunity to make those decisions herself and exercise her judgment. All Parties are willing to meet, exchange information, ask questions and come up with proposals but Mr. Siracusa asked for an interim order. Counsel went over monthly expenses/income of the properties and came up with a $1,500.00 monthly deficit. The Amended Third Accountings were granted (subject to review of the order). The Petition for Instructions was continued to November 19, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with an interim order that will hold Mr. Thomas harmless for not making further payment for Suisun and Hercules prior to November 19, 2012 subject to nothing arising that would make the withholding of payments irreparable and on the premise that the Parties will get together to reach a plan of action. Mr. Siracusa is to prepare, circulate and submit the order.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
